blockingIssues.description: lib/match-details.ts returns an unavailable payload with a cache-miss label even when a raw match record exists but is incomplete, so the details fallback reason is not truthful for partial-data cases.

vitest.config.ts only includes lib/**/*.test.ts, so the new app/api/match-details/route.test.ts file is never executed by pnpm test and the feature's claimed route-level automated coverage is ineffective.

appliedUpdates.description: Documented match-details raw source precedence and the need for truthful missing-vs-partial fallback semantics in .factory/library/architecture.md.

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: Add explicit Windows-safe guidance for setup steps that currently mention .factory/init.sh so workers do not attempt to run the POSIX shell script directly on this mission host.
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: The search-saved-lookups-and-route-synchronization review noted the worker had to work around a failed .factory/init.sh attempt on Windows, and .factory/library/environment.md already records that the mission host is Windows.

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: Warn workers that the committed Vitest config currently only includes lib/**/*.test.ts and they must either keep tests within that scope or update the committed test configuration when adding route/component tests.
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: The match-details-ui-and-flow review found that app/api/match-details/route.test.ts is outside vitest.config.ts include globs, so pnpm test did not run the new test despite the worker handoff claiming route coverage.

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: Reinforce that web-feature workers must run the committed lint/static-check gate in addition to typecheck/tests/build before handoff unless the orchestrator explicitly waives it.
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: The match-card-metadata-and-truthful-rank review found the worker handoff omitted pnpm run lint/static-check even though .factory/services.yaml and the skill both describe lint/static-check as required validation gates.
